summary:
The patient presents with bilateral upper limb jerks and occasional neck twitches indicating symmetric, non-focal movement—consistent with multifocal clonic seizures due to generalized involvement across both cerebral hemispheres. This pattern rules out localized focal seizures and supports a diagnosis aligned with generalized seizure types such as those described under option B.  

Note: Option B refers specifically to multifocal clonic seizures which match the symmetry and spread of neurological signs reported here.
